question_text: Let $X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } , \dots , X _ { n }$ be a random sample of size $n \left( \geq 1 1 \right)$ from a population with continuous and strictly increasing cumulative distribution function $F ( \cdot )$ with an unknown median ??. To test $H _ { 0 } \colon M = 1 0$ against $H _ { 1 } \colon M > 1 0$ at level $\alpha ,$ let the statistic $T$ denote the number of observations larger than 10. Let $t _ { 0 }$ be the observed value of the test statistic ??. Consider the test which rejects $H _ { 0 }$ if $T \geq c$ Then the ??-value of the test is

options.A: $ \sum _ { i = t _ { 0 } } ^ { n } { \frac { n ! } { i ! ( n - i ) ! } } ( 0 . 5 ) ^ { n }\tag{B} $ $ \sum _ { i = 1 0 } ^ { n } { \frac { n ! } { i ! ( n - i ) ! } } ( 0 . 5 ) ^ { n }\tag{C} $ $ \sum _ { i = 0 } ^ { 1 0 } { \frac { n ! } { i ! \left( n - i \right) ! } } \left( 0 . 5 \right) ^ { n }\tag{D} $ $ \sum _ { i = 0 } ^ { t _ { 0 } } \frac { n ! } { i ! \left( n - i \right) ! } \left( 0 . 5 \right) ^ { n } $

solution:
To determine the p-value of the test, we need to understand the distribution of the test statistic \( T \) under the null hypothesis \( H_0: M = 10 \). Since \( F(x) \) is the cumulative distribution function of the population and it is strictly increasing, the probability that any given observation \( X_i \) is greater than 10 is \( P(X_i > 10) = 1 - F(10) = 0.5 \) under \( H_0 \).

The test statistic \( T \) is the number of observations in the sample that are greater than 10. Under \( H_0 \), \( T \) follows a binomial distribution with parameters \( n \) and \( p = 0.5 \). The probability mass function of a binomial random variable \( T \) is given by:

\[ P(T = k) = \binom{n}{k} (0.5)^k (0.5)^{n-k} = \binom{n}{k} (0.5)^n \]

The p-value of the test is the probability of observing a value of \( T \) as extreme or more extreme than the observed value \( t_0 \) under the null hypothesis. Since the alternative hypothesis \( H_1: M > 10 \) is a one-sided test, we reject \( H_0 \) if \( T \geq t_0 \). Therefore, the p-value is:

\[ \text{p-value} = P(T \geq t_0) = \sum_{i=t_0}^n \binom{n}{i} (0.5)^n \]

Thus, the correct option is:

\[
\boxed{A}
\]
